i need to decompose the problem into four independent modules. the first module will handle the calculation of absolute differences for non-multiple-of-3 indices. the second module will handle the calculation of squared differences for multiple-of-3 indices. the third module will manage the overall summation for each element. the fourth module will ensure the constraints are met and the output is formatted correctly.

the first module will calculate the absolute differences for elements that are not multiples of 3. this involves iterating through the array and summing the absolute differences between the current element and all others. the second module will handle the squared differences for elements that are multiples of 3. this involves iterating through the array and summing the squared differences between the current element and all others. the third module will combine the results from the first and second modules to form the final result array. the fourth module will ensure that the output meets all the constraints, including the correct formatting and the use of the letter k at least twice.
